uplink.description: Battery, temperature, and humidity (TTN example bytes)
Negative temperature (synthetic, exercises signed LE decode)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input; expected derived via the oracle method (reference/upstream-codec.js): battery 80%, temp 0xFFF6 LE = -10/10 = -1.0 C, humidity 90/2 = 45%.
Synthetic in-bounds input; expected derived via the oracle method (reference/upstream-codec.js): battery 72%, temp 0x00DD LE = 221/10 = 22.1 C, humidity 84/2 = 42%.

uplink.description: Current reading plus one datalog history point (synthetic)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input exercising the 0x20/0xce datalog channel; expected derived via the oracle method (reference/upstream-codec.js), which yields current temp 25.0/hum 50 and a history point timestamp 1699291264 (-> 2023-11-06T17:21:04.000Z), temp 0x0128 LE = 296/10 = 29.6 C, humidity 90/2 = 45%. Upstream emits the prior reading in a flat `history` array with a raw `timestamp`; we normalize each entry to vocabulary keys with an RFC3339 `time` per AUTHORING.md.
Synthetic input with an unrecognized channel id (0x09); the reference/upstream-codec.js recognizes nothing and returns an empty object, so our codec reports an error.
